open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js loadDocument() load boundary, and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's new module reverse-pages.js (TASK-350) is NOT a new boundary — it reads the ALREADY-OPEN, already-validated document via doc.getData() and uploads nothing, so it adds no fresh ingest surface. All remaining gaps are client-side-only DoS (uploader's own tab) on the 1.6 GiB box. Single right developer fix remains: page-count cap once at viewer load (covers all per-page consumers) + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles(). No server-side risk.

notes: 40 JS modules (+1 since 12:30 review: reverse-pages.js, TASK-350 VERIFIED). reverse-pages.js (single-click: build a new PDF with pages in descending order n-1..0 and download; 1-page doc reverses to itself) is security CLEAN: no new upload boundary — operates on the open viewer doc's own bytes via pdf.js doc.getData(), builds a FRESH pdf-lib doc (open doc never mutated), nothing leaves the browser; filename sanitized [^a-zA-Z0-9._-]->_ slice(180) -> <base>_reversed.pdf; status via textContent only (never innerHTML); safe Blob download + setTimeout URL.revokeObjectURL(1s); guards for no-doc/numPages==0 and window.PDFLib-unready; errors caught -> inline status + EventBus.ERROR; talks to app only through EventBus (PDF_LOADED/PDF_CLEARED) + ActionRegistry (reverse.run).
